### Repo summary

- Playwright (TypeScript) end-to-end test project using `@playwright/test` (devDependency: ^1.56.1).
- Tests live in `tests/` (organized into subfolders like `Inputs/`, `Login/`, `Register/`).
- Page objects follow a lightweight POM pattern under `pages/` (example: `pages/practice.expandtesting.com-inputs.ts` exports `InputsPage` with `navigate()` and `submitFormWithValues(...)`).
- Playwright config: `playwright.config.ts` — `testDir: './tests'`, `reporter: 'html'`, `trace: 'on-first-retry'`, `screenshot: 'only-on-failure'`.

### Quick commands (exact for this repo)

- Run all tests: `npx playwright test`
- Run a single test file: `npx playwright test tests/Inputs/inputs.test.ts`
- Run a specific project (from `playwright.config.ts`): `npx playwright test --project=firefox`
- Open the HTML report generated by Playwright: `npx playwright show-report` (report output goes to `playwright-report/` by default)

Notes: `package.json` has no custom scripts; use `npx playwright ...` or add scripts if you want shorthand commands.

### Patterns & conventions an AI should follow

- Page objects: classes in `pages/` export a default class that accepts a Playwright `Page` in the constructor. Methods are small and imperative (e.g., `navigate()`, `submitFormWithValues(data)`). Use these POMs from tests rather than querying selectors ad-hoc.
  - Example: `InputsPage` exposes `inputNumber`, `inputText`, `displayInputsButton` and a `submitFormWithValues({ number, text, password, date })` helper — tests call those helpers.
- Tests directory: use the `tests/` folder layout already present. Keep test files in subfolders that indicate feature scope (Inputs, Login, Register).
- TypeScript: files are `.ts`. The repo relies on Playwright's TypeScript support; avoid adding a separate build step unless tests or new tooling require it.

### What to change when adding features

- New page objects: add a file in `pages/` named after the site/feature (follow existing naming style like `practice.expandtesting.com-*.ts`).
- New tests: place them under `tests/<FeatureName>/` and reference the page object via `import PageObject from '../../pages/<filename>'` or an appropriate relative path.
- If you need reusable test utilities, add `utils/` or `helpers/` at repo root and import from tests. Keep Playwright-specific helpers (trace/screenshot handling) aligned with `playwright.config.ts` settings.

### Debugging & CI hints

- Local debugging: run `npx playwright test -p firefox --debug` or use `npx playwright test --headed` to observe actions.
- CI: `playwright.config.ts` already reads `process.env.CI` to set `forbidOnly`, `retries`, and `workers`. Keep that behavior when adding CI jobs.

### Integration & external dependencies

- Main dependency: `@playwright/test` (dev only). Node types present via `@types/node`.
- No other external services are configured in the repo (no `.env` usage enabled in `playwright.config.ts`, but commented example shows how to add dotenv).

### Examples of project-specific idioms for code generation

- When creating page objects: follow existing Locator naming (e.g., `inputNumber`, `displayInputsButton`, `outputNumber`) and expose both locators and small high-level actions (e.g., `submitFormWithValues`).
- When writing tests: import the page class, instantiate with the Playwright `page` fixture, call `navigate()` then high-level helpers instead of repeating selector logic.

### If you modify package.json or CI

- If you add test scripts, place them under `scripts` (e.g., `"test": "npx playwright test"`) to make common commands discoverable.

---
If any of these sections are unclear or you want more examples (for instance, an example test using `InputsPage`), tell me what to include and I will iterate.
